topic: Theme 4: The Franco Dictatorship and Transition to Democracy
subject: spanish

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Spain' in Spanish?
items.why: Students drop the 'a' or use a conjugated verb. The near future is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is obligatory: voy A comer, vamos A ver, vas A estudiar. Heavily tested for holiday/future plans.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: How do you say 'I used the computer today'? (Spain Spanish)
items.why: In Spain, HOY (today) and recent time markers (esta semana, este mes) trigger PRETÉRITO PERFECTO (he + participle). Latin America uses preterite for these. Common error in essays: 'esta mañana fui' instead of 'esta mañana he ido'.
It's seamos. “somos” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Tejero. “Suárez” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was the failed 23-F coup attempt?
items.why: The real figure is 1981. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
